mnemonics: Gradient Lineup: Nabla (∇) acts on scalars, gives vectors. Treat Nabla like a vector that differentiates.
keyPoints: The gradient of a scalar function f, ∇f, points in the direction of the steepest ascent.
The magnitude of the gradient, |∇f|, represents the rate of change in that steepest direction.
The gradient operator (∇) follows the product rule: ∇(fg) = f∇g + g∇f.
commonMistakes: Applying the gradient operator (∇) to a vector field incorrectly; ∇f is for scalars, divergence (∇·) and curl (∇×) are for vectors.
Forgetting the product rule for gradients and mistakenly assuming ∇(fg) = (∇f)(∇g).
